poster_title: Take a Step Back: Evoking Reasoning via Abstraction in Large Language Models
authors: Huaixiu Steven Zheng, Swaroop Mishra, Xinyun Chen, Heng-Tze Cheng, Ed H. Chi, Quoc V Le, Denny Zhou

content: LLMs have improved NLP tasks but struggle with complex reasoning involving low-level details.
Intermediate reasoning steps often introduce errors, reducing overall model accuracy.
Need for a method to enhance reasoning by abstracting high-level concepts.
LLMs face challenges in multi-step reasoning tasks.
Errors in intermediate steps can propagate and affect final results.
Current prompting techniques are not sufficient to handle complex reasoning.
STEP-BACK PROMPTING: Two-step technique of abstraction and reasoning.
Abstraction: Derive high-level concepts from the original question.
Reasoning: Use high-level concepts to guide the reasoning process.
panels.figure_caption: Strong Performance of STEP-BACK PROMPTING: our proposed Abstraction-and-Reasoning

content: Conducted using PaLM-2L, GPT-4, and Llama2-70B models.
Tasks: MMLU (Physics and Chemistry), TimeQA, and MuSiQue.
Compared with chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting.
7% and 11% improvement on MMLU (Physics and Chemistry) for PaLM-2L.
27% performance gain on TimeQA and 7% on MuSiQue.
Up to 36% performance improvement over CoT prompting.
STEP-BACK PROMPTING reduces errors in intermediate reasoning steps.
The technique fixes a large portion of errors (up to 40%) while introducing few new errors (max 12%).
Ablation studies show the effectiveness of the method in various tasks.
panels.figure_caption: Ablation and error analysis of STEP-BACK PROMPTING on TimeQA

content: STEP-BACK PROMPTING is a promising approach to enhance reasoning in LLMs.
The technique grounds reasoning on high-level concepts, reducing errors.
Future work should focus on improving LLM reasoning capabilities.
